Guide.Description: The Air Half life (CORAL) is a one-variable model based on 2D descriptor and on experiments with Air Half life.
Guide.Model:
The model has been developed by: Andrey Toropov, Istituto di Ricerche Farmacologiche Mario Negri IRCSS, Via Mario Negri 2, 20156 Milano, Italy (andrey.toropov@marionegri.it) https://www.marionegri.it/ Alla Toropova, Istituto di Ricerche Farmacologiche Mario Negri IRCSS, Via Mario Negri 2, 20156 Milano, Italy (alla.toropova@marionegri.it) https://www.marionegri.it/ Giovanna J. Lavado, Istituto di Ricerche Farmacologiche Mario Negri IRCSS, Via Mario Negri 2, 20156 Milano, Italy (giovanna.lavado@marionegri.it) https://www.marionegri.it/
Date of model development and/or publication: July 9, 2019
Guide.Dataset: The model is based on experiments with Air Half life Endpoint units: Half life =log(hours) Dependent variable: Log(hours) Experimental protocol: NA Endpoint data quality and variability: NA Pre-processing of data before modelling: The SMILES were pre-processed using the KNIME software. Availability of the training set: Yes Data for the dependent variable for the training set: All Availability of the external validation set: Yes Data for the dependent variable for the external validation set: All Other information about the training set: The original total set of compounds has been randomly split into the training (75%) and validation (25%) sets. Other information about the external validation set: NA
Guide.Descriptors:
Type of model: One variable model based on 2D descriptor Explicit algorithm: Monte Carlo method based on CORAL descriptors. Endpoint = 2.2271129+ 0.0900047 × DCW(1,15) Algorithm and descriptor generation: The Monte Carlo Method for the optimization, and SMILES attributes as descriptors. Software name and version for descriptor generation: CORAL 2019 (modified), Istituto di Ricerche Farmacologiche Mario Negri IRCCS, 20156 Milano, Italy http://www.insilico.eu/coral/
The model uses optimal 2D descriptors.
Descriptors in the model: Applied the correlation weights for standard SMILES attributes S, SS, and SSS together with the correlation weights of HARD codes. Descriptor selection: 2D optimal descriptor 𝐷𝐶𝑊(𝑇,𝑁)=𝐶𝑊(𝐻𝐴𝑅𝐷)+Σ𝐶𝑊(𝑆𝑘)+Σ𝐶𝑊(𝑆𝑆𝑘)+Σ𝐶𝑊(𝑆𝑆𝑆𝑘) Algorithm and descriptor generation: The Monte Carlo Method for the optimization, and SMILES attributes as descriptors. Software name and version for descriptor generation: CORAL 2019 (modified), Istituto di Ricerche Farmacologiche Mario Negri IRCCS, 20156 Milano, Italy http://www.insilico.eu/coral/

AD.Name: Similar_Molecules
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: strongly similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: only moderately similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: no similar compounds with known experimental value in the training set have been found

AD.Name: Accuracy
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is good
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not optimal
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: accuracy of prediction for similar molecules found in the training set is not adequate

AD.Name: Concordance
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that agree with the target compound predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that slightly disagree with the target compound predicted value
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: similar molecules found in the training set have experimental values that completely disagree with the target compound predicted value

AD.Name: Maximum_Error
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: the maximum error in prediction of similar molecules found in the training set has a low value, considering the experimental variability
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: the maximum error in prediction of similar molecules found in the training set has a moderate value, considering the experimental variability
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: the maximum error in prediction of similar molecules found in the training set has a high value, considering the experimental variability

AD.Name: Model_Descriptors_Range_Check
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: descriptors for this compound have values inside the descriptor range of the compounds of the training set
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: descriptors for this compound have values outside the descriptor range of the compounds of the training set

AD.Name: ACF_SimCheck
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: all atom centered fragment of the compound have been found in the compounds of the training set
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: some atom centered fragment of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: a prominent number of atom centered fragments of the compound have not been found in the compounds of the training set or are rare fragments

AD.Name: AD_Index
AD.DescriptionRangeTop: predicted substance is into the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeMid: predicted substance could be out of the Applicability Domain of the model
AD.DescriptionRangeBottom: predicted substance is out of the the Applicability Domain of the model

xml.Reference:
It has been described here: Toropov A., Toropova A., Roncaglioni A., Benfenati E., Prediction of biochemical endpoints by the CORAL software: Prejudices, Paradoxes, and Results. (2018) Methods in Molecular Biology, 1800, pp. 573-583. https://link.springer.com/protocol/10.1007%2F978-1-4939-7899-1_27 A.P. Toropova, A.A. Toropov, A. Lombardo, G. Lavado, and E. Benfenati, Paradox of "ideal correlations": improved model for air half-life of persistent organic pollutants. Environmental Technology, Accepted Jan 22, 2021. DOI: 10.1080/09593330.2021.1882588
For a general description of the VEGA software see: Benfenati E, Manganaro A, Gini G. VEGA-QSAR: AI inside a platform for predictive toxicology Proceedings of the workshop "Popularize Artificial Intelligence 2013", December 5th 2013, Turin, Italy Published on CEUR Workshop Proceedings Vol-1107
xml.Endpoint: The endpoint is the phototrasformation in Air (half life calculated as log(hours)) according to ENV FATE 5.1.1 guidance.
